question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getFees()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /api/v1/network/fees with a timestamp range where lower > upper (e.g., ?timestamp=gt:9999999999&timestamp=lt:1), causing Bound construction to throw an IllegalArgumentException that, if the global error handler returns a verbose stack trace, leaks internal implementation details useful for further exploitation?
